edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback has 1 ACCEPT at 8:1 R:R on 1h (PF=1.442, 156 trades, ranging PF=1.795). THESIS: The pullback mechanism behaves DIFFERENTLY across timeframes. On 15m, pullbacks are RAPID — driven by order flow imbalance and liquidity sweeps that resolve in 1-3 bars. On 4h, pullbacks are STRUCTURAL — representing genuine repositioning after multi-day trend continuation. This creates a natural R:R LADDER: shorter timeframes need lower R:R (quicker resolution) while longer timeframes support higher R:R (longer trend continuation after the pullback). Testing: 8:1 on 15m (fast snap-back), 10:1 on 1h (proven timeframe with higher R:R), 12:1 on 4h (structural pullback, maximum R:R). The diagnostic question: does PF INCREASE with timeframe? If yes, the mechanism is genuine and 4h pullbacks are the highest-quality signals. If PF is constant across timeframes, the edge is timeframe-invariant and we should maximize trade count (15m). If PF decreases with timeframe, 1h is the sweet spot and the mechanism doesn't scale. INVALIDATION: If all three variants produce PF < 1.0, the RSI pullback edge has been arbitraged away since the original ACCEPT was generated.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_15m_8to1_snapback
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/15m at 8:1 R:R. The SPEED variant. On 15m, RSI oscillates rapidly — a pullback below 30 (oversold) and recovery above 30 happens frequently as intrabar noise pushes RSI to extremes. With EMA_50 as trend filter, only pullbacks in the direction of the 15m trend are taken. At 1.5x ATR stop, the position is killed quickly if the pullback was actually a reversal (not just noise). At 8:1 TP (12.0x ATR), the target is a 2-4 hour continuation of the 15m trend. Expected: 300-500 trades. High trade count + 8:1 R:R = robust statistical sample. If PF > 1.2, this is a viable high-frequency ranging strategy. Never backtested: rsi_pullback on 15m is completely unexplored.
entry_long: close > EMA_50 (bullish trend — pullback in uptrend context).
RSI_14 crosses above oversold level (RSI recovery from pullback).
entry_short: close < EMA_50 (bearish trend — pullback in downtrend context).
RSI_14 crosses below overbought level (RSI rejection from pullback).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
EMA baseline: close vs EMA_50 for trend direction
RSI confirmation: RSI_14 pullback recovery from oversold/overbought
ATR volatility: position sizing and stop/TP calculation
15m timeframe: fast pullback resolution, high trade count
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
15m snap-back: does the pullback resolve within 2-4 hours?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_10to1_extended
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h at 10:1 R:R. The EXTENDED R:R variant on the proven timeframe. The existing ACCEPT runs at 8:1 (PF=1.442). Pushing to 10:1 tests whether the 1h pullback mechanism supports larger moves. At 10:1 R:R with 1.5x ATR stop, the TP is 15.0x ATR — targeting the FULL continuation of the trend leg after the pullback resolves. If PF > 1.442, the original 8:1 is leaving money on the table. If PF drops to ~1.2, the extra 2x ATR of TP target catches diminishing returns. Expected: ~120-150 trades (slightly fewer than 156 because some 8:1 winners don't reach 10:1).
entry_long: close > EMA_50 (bullish trend — pullback in uptrend context).
RSI_14 crosses above oversold level (RSI recovery from pullback).
entry_short: close < EMA_50 (bearish trend — pullback in downtrend context).
RSI_14 crosses below overbought level (RSI rejection from pullback).

variants.name: rsi_pullback_4h_12to1_structural
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h at 12:1 R:R. The STRUCTURAL PULLBACK variant. On 4h, an RSI pullback to oversold represents 8+ hours of sustained selling pressure within an intact trend. This is NOT noise — it's institutional repositioning. When RSI recovers from this deep pullback, it signals that buying pressure has absorbed the selling wave. At 12:1 R:R with 1.5x ATR stop, each winner captures a multi-day trend continuation (18.0x ATR). Expected: 30-60 trades. Few trades but highest conviction per signal. If PF > 1.3, 4h RSI pullbacks are the highest-quality version of this template. 12:1 R:R is validated (RSI slingshot PF=1.270 at 12:1).
entry_long: RSI_14 crosses above oversold level (recovery from structural pullback).
entry_short: RSI_14 crosses below overbought level (rejection from structural rally).
